Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a given event log of an Insurance Claims Processing process identify cases with performance issues long durations deduce root causes based on specific attributes Resource Region Complexity explain why these attributes cause delays and suggest mitigations

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Data Extraction Processing Mental or Scratchpad Case 2001 Start 2024 04 01 09 00 End 2024 04 01 10 30 Duration 1 hour 30 mins Region A Complexity Low Docs requested 0 Case 2002 Start 2024 04 01 09 05 End 2024 04 02 11 00 Duration 26 hours Region B Complexity Medium Docs requested 1 Case 2003 Start 2024 04 01 09 10 End 2024 04 03 09 30 Duration 48 hours 20 mins Region A Complexity High Docs requested 2 Case 2004 Start 2024 04 01 09 20 End 2024 04 01 10 45 Duration 1 hour 25 mins Region B Complexity Low Docs requested 0 Case 2005 Start 2024 04 01 09 25 End 2024 04 04 14 30 Duration 77 hours 5 mins Region B Complexity High Docs requested 3

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Task 1 Identify Cases with Performance Issues Fast cases 2001 1 5h 2004 1 4h These are Low complexity Moderate case 2002 26h Medium complexity Slow Problematic cases 2003 48h and 2005 77h These are significantly longer than the baseline and are clearly the ones with performance issues

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Complexity The strongest correlation Low fast less than 2h Medium moderate 26h High slow 48h to 77h High complexity inherently takes longer

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region Lets look closer Region A Case 2001 Low fast Case 2003 High slow Region B Case 2004 Low fast Case 2002 Medium moderate Case 2005 High very slow Is Region the issue Not directly Low complexity cases are fast in both regions However compare the High complexity cases between regions Case 2003 Region A took 48h Case 2005 Region B took 77h Region B seems to struggle more with high complexity

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Resource Lets look at who handles the slow cases 2003 and 2005 Case 2003 CSR Jane Submit Close Adjuster Mike Evaluate Doc requests x2 Manager Bill Approve Finance Alan Pay Case 2005 CSR Paul Submit Close Adjuster Lisa Evaluate Doc requests x3 Manager Bill Approve Finance Carl Pay

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Adjuster Mike Region A handles High complexity in 30h Submit to Approve with 2 doc requests Adjuster Lisa Region B handles High complexity in 84h Submit to Approve with 3 doc requests Adjuster Lisa is clearly struggling more with High complexity cases than Adjuster Mike requiring an extra document request loop

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Manager Bill approves both slow cases 2003 and 2005 Lets check Manager Anns cases 2001 2002 2004 All faster Wait look at the approval wait times for Manager Bill Case 2003 Last doc request Apr 1 17 00 to Approval Apr 2 16 00 23 hours wait Case 2005 Last doc request Apr 3 15 00 to Approval Apr 4 10 00 19 hours wait Manager Ann approvals Case 2001 0h Case 2002 20h but includes overnight Case 2004 0h

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesis of Root Causes 1 Complexity Primary Driver High complexity cases take exponentially longer due to the Request Additional Documents loop 2 Resource Secondary Driver Adjuster Lisa requires more document request iterations for high complexity cases compared to Adjuster Mike Manager Bill seems to have longer approval processing times even post document gathering compared to Manager Ann 3 Region Tertiary Contextual Driver Region Bs performance degrades significantly on High complexity claims compared to Region A likely due to the specific resources operating there Adjuster Lisa Manager Bill

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Complexity High complexity means more ambiguous claims Why Lack of initial documentation unclear liability and so on Mitigation Better upfront triage dynamic checklists for submission dedicated high complexity lanes Resource Adjuster Lisa Asking for docs piecemeal 3 requests vs 2 Why Poor communication lack of experience with complex claims not giving a complete list upfront Mitigation Training standardizing document request templates mentorship from Adjuster Mike Resource Manager Bill Delays in approval Why Approval bottleneck lack of delegation heavy workload Mitigation Clearer SLAs for approvals empowering adjusters to auto approve low medium reviewing Bills capacity Region Region B Combination of the above Mitigation Cross regional knowledge sharing balancing workload between regions

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Structure and Refine the Output Use clear headings matching the prompts tasks Start with a summary of the case durations to justify the problem cases Present the analysis logically Complexity to Resource to Region Make the Why and How to fix sections actionable and professional Use business process mining terminology such as rework loops bottleneck SLA piecemeal requests

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction Review during drafting Did I do the math right 2001 09 00 to 10 30 1 5 hrs 2002 09 05 to 11 00 next day 26 hrs includes overnight 1 doc request 2003 09 10 to 09 30 third day 48 hrs 2 doc requests 2004 09 20 to 10 45 1 4 hrs 2005 09 25 to 14 30 fourth day 77 hrs 3 doc requests Is Region strictly at fault No Region B handles Low complexity fine Case 2004 I must clarify that Region is a proxy for the resources working in that region not a geographical delay itself Make sure to highlight the Request Additional Documents activity This is the obvious process bottleneck for long cases Its a rework loop

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Check tone readability and ensure all constraints of the prompt are met The resulting output matches the provided good response
